section: P1.1 Establish The Evaluation Set First

Keep it inside the versioned heldout scorer — registry_eval 'scorer' row warns of three parallel eval scorers with no shared code, plus a stale 'scorer v2' comment at run_heldout_eval.py:45 while HEAD is v3.

section: P1.1 Establish The Evaluation Set First

section: P1.1 Establish The Evaluation Set First

Build ONE harness, not three one-offs. Needed no later than S8.

Always qualify.

section: P1.2 Activate The Grounded Planner Safely
verbatim: Selective trigger logic exists.
action: Re-verify should_run_grounded_planner (grounded_planner.py:120) exists and returns False on strong deterministic plans — confirmed as a symbol via registry_retrieval; add/keep a unit test pinning the trigger contract.
why: selective triggering is what keeps planner cost and drift bounded
feasibility_note: Symbol verified; note the ledger [x] covers existence only — trigger QUALITY (weak-confidence keying) is the separate open claim P1Q:L594.
required_receipt: pytest asserting should_run_grounded_planner returns False for a confident deterministic plan and True for a weak one, green

section: P1.2 Activate The Grounded Planner Safely
verbatim: Block production activation until deterministic librarian_card.v0, shelf scoring, seat policy, and the held-out baseline are operational.
action: Keep the activation block standing and auditable: verify the planner stays dark (provider calls disabled unless explicitly enabled — grounded_planner.py module contract; no provider/model configured in config.py) until (a) librarian_card.v0 rebuilt post-capture at S8, (b) shelf scoring live, (c) seat policy A/B'd, (d) held-out baseline harness green; owner flips the flag only with all four receipts in hand.
who: owner (activation decision); script verifies the block each deploy
why: activating an LLM expander before deterministic gates and baselines exist would make its gains unmeasurable and its drift invisible
feasibility_note: The block is currently in effect: grep of config.py finds no GROUNDED_PLANNER model/provider settings, and grounded_planner.py:5 states provider calls are disabled unless explicitly enabled. Two of four prerequisites (card.v0 exists, held-out baseline captured) are real but S8 rebuild supersedes today's cards (critique C2: pair cards built with empty latent feed).
required_receipt: config dump showing planner disabled at every deploy until an owner-signed activation note lists receipts for all four prerequisites

section: P1.2 Activate The Grounded Planner Safely
verbatim: Configure an explicit planner provider/model and validate the durable call budget.
action: Add explicit grounded-planner provider/model settings to config (grep finds none today) and validate the durable call budget end-to-end: bounded canary run exercising the grounded_query_planner_usage lease, including refusal at budget 0.
why: an unconfigured provider means every activation attempt silently no-ops or falls back — the silent-fallback class
feasibility_note: Budget-lease machinery already exists (grounded_query_planner_usage, grounded_planner.py:28); only the provider/model config and its validation canary are missing. No explicit S row exists for P1.2 activation — earliest honest slot is S8 (all four L590 prerequisites real); librarian Phase 4 'planner-when-thin' is the same activation under another name.
required_receipt: canary log showing configured model id, provider_calls==1 counted, budget decremented, and a refused call at budget 0

section: P1.2 Activate The Grounded Planner Safely
verbatim: Trigger only when deterministic vocabulary/plan confidence is weak or the query requires grounded cross-domain expansion.
action: Implement the trigger condition: key should_run_grounded_planner off deterministic QueryPlanV2 vocabulary/plan-confidence signals and a grounded cross-domain-expansion need; emit trigger-rate diagnostics; measure trigger precision on the held-out suite.
why: firing the LLM only on weak-confidence/cross-domain queries is what keeps expert queries fast and unregressed
feasibility_note: TERMINOLOGY: 'plan confidence' lives on the DETERMINISTIC query planner (query_plan.py) — registry_retrieval's collision note requires qualifying deterministic query planner vs LLM grounded planner; this claim is the bridge between the two and must consume, not duplicate, QueryPlanV2 signals.
required_receipt: diagnostics on a held-out run showing planner fired only on weak-confidence/cross-domain rows, with trigger rate and example ids in the EVAL artifact

section: P1.2 Activate The Grounded Planner Safely
verbatim: Preserve original-query and exact-string lanes.

why: mirrors the non-negotiable invariant that the original query remains a protected lane and expansion cannot drift it
feasibility_note: Mechanism exists dark (registry_retrieval: grounded_planner_lanes 'stamps original_lane_protected=True (line 646)'; 'protected lane' canonical = protected_recall_lane, role=='original'). Ledger box stays open only because activation-time live proof is pending — the code side is built.
required_receipt: pytest injecting a hostile plan and asserting original + exact-string lanes survive fusion unmodified, green

section: P1.2 Activate The Grounded Planner Safely
verbatim: Reject introduced terms without selected-corpus lexicon IDs.
action: Re-verify the rejection path already in code: _validate_plan rejects introduced terms lacking a scoped lexicon citation (grounded_planner.py:148-206, reason 'missing_question_or_scoped_lexicon_citation'; validated terms carry lexicon_entry_id, :183-206); add a unit test with an off-lexicon term and confirm rejected_expansions surfaces it.
why: lexicon-ID citation is the groundedness contract — without it the planner is a free-associating expander
feasibility_note: Verified today by direct grep: the prompt contract ('Every introduced domain term must cite one supplied lexicon_entry_id', grounded_planner.py:58) and the validator both exist. Ledger 'open' overstates the gap — remaining work is an asserting test + live activation receipt, not a build.
required_receipt: unit test green (off-lexicon term lands in rejected_expansions, never in lanes) + activation canary showing rejected_expansions populated

section: P1.2 Activate The Grounded Planner Safely
verbatim: Log provider calls, cache hits, cited concept IDs, rejected expansions, latency, and token usage.
action: Close the logging gap: provider_calls, cache_hit, rejected_expansions, and cited lexicon_entry_ids are already emitted (grounded_planner.py:246,312-459; surfaced via vocabulary_diagnostics.grounded_planner, chat_orchestrator.py:6260-6328); add latency and token-usage counters, then verify all six fields on a live canary call.
why: silent-fallback accounting: every planner call must count and surface its cost and its rejections
feasibility_note: Four of six fields verified in code today; only latency and token usage are missing (max_tokens=650 is set but usage is not recorded). Small, additive change on an existing diagnostics surface.

section: P1.2 Activate The Grounded Planner Safely
verbatim: Lay-query concept/document recall improves materially over baseline.
action: Run the planner-on vs planner-off A/B on the held-out suite (naive/lay + cross_domain shape subset) using the compare harness; require doc/concept recall gain above a preregistered threshold.
who: script: run_heldout_eval.py x2 + compare harness; owner sets the threshold in advance
why: 'materially improves' must be a number set before the run, not after (hypothesis-promotion rule)
feasibility_note: Blocked only by the L583 compare harness and L592 provider config. Note registry_eval: 'preregistered thresholds' is PLAN-ONLY — the phrase appears nowhere in the repo, so the threshold must be written down (ledger or eval config) before the A/B.
required_receipt: EVAL diff artifact: naive/lay subset recall delta >= preregistered threshold, planner-on vs off, same question-id subset

section: P1.2 Activate The Grounded Planner Safely
verbatim: Direct expert queries do not regress.
action: Same A/B as L603, scored on the direct/expert subset: require zero material regression in doc-hit and latency for direct queries with the planner enabled.
why: the planner must be pure upside — expert queries already work and must not pay for lay-query gains
feasibility_note: The selective trigger (L594) is the mechanism that makes this achievable: direct queries with confident deterministic plans should never invoke the planner at all, making regression structurally unlikely if the trigger is correct.
required_receipt: EVAL diff artifact: direct-shape subset doc-hit delta >= 0 and latency delta within preregistered bound

section: P1.2 Activate The Grounded Planner Safely
verbatim: Planner drift cannot satisfy a required lane without source evidence.
action: Add an adversarial test: inject fabricated planner terms/lanes and assert no required lane can be satisfied without source evidence — i.e. uncited terms are rejected (L597 path) and lane satisfaction requires evidence-backed, lexicon-cited members only.
why: mirrors the invariant that only deterministic evidence-backed policy grants seats — the LLM proposes, evidence disposes
feasibility_note: The enforcement primitives exist (_validate_plan rejection + protected lanes + deterministic seat policy); the missing piece is the adversarial assertion tying them together as an acceptance receipt.
required_receipt: pytest: hostile plan with fabricated citations cannot mark any required lane satisfied; green

section: P1.3 Add Conversation And Open-Book Anchoring
verbatim: Distinguish session context from persistent user preferences.
action: Design and implement the separation: session anchors live in conversation state (services/conversation.py) and expire with the session; persistent preferences live in a new versioned user-profile store with an explicit write path; document the lifecycle of each.
where: backend/services/conversation.py; backend/services/chat_orchestrator.py; new Mongo user-profile collection
why: conflating session context with durable profile is how silent preference persistence (the L620 hazard) happens
feasibility_note: TERMINOLOGY: canonical for P1.3 is conversation_anchoring (registry_retrieval, PLAN-ONLY — zero code hits for session/conversation/accepted anchors; all 8 P1.3 boxes open). 'Anchor' collides four ways: (1) this, (2) DocumentAnchorRetriever title lane (exists), (3) graph anchor entities, (4) summary key_point child anchors. Do not overload sense (2)'s names.
required_receipt: schema doc + test: session anchor gone after session end, profile signal persists and appears in the inspection API

section: P1.3 Add Conversation And Open-Book Anchoring
verbatim: Allow users to inspect, edit, or disable persistent profile signals.
action: Expose profile signals via API (list/edit/disable/delete) and a frontend surface; default new signal classes to visible; write a frontend e2e covering inspect→edit→disable.
where: backend API routes; frontend at :3000 (rebuild container to see edits); new profile collection
why: user control over persistent signals is the consent mechanism that makes personalization defensible
feasibility_note: Straightforward CRUD + UI; depends on L609's store existing first. Frontend e2e machinery already exists (live-trace-tabs.spec.ts pattern, localStorage polymath-auth injection).
required_receipt: e2e spec green: signals listed, edited, disabled; disabled signal verifiably absent from the next retrieval's diagnostics

section: P1.3 Add Conversation And Open-Book Anchoring
verbatim: Pass accepted document/source anchors into follow-up retrieval.
action: Feed accepted document/source anchors from conversation state into follow-up retrieval as candidate priors, reusing the existing anchor-confidence machinery (ranking_policy.py:91-95 gates) with a session-carryover feed rather than a new ranker.
why: follow-ups that forget the open book force users to restate context and break the librarian illusion
feasibility_note: registry_retrieval anchoring row: 'anchors-as-priors machinery already exists per-query and needs a session-carryover feed, not a new ranker' — the design cost is the feed contract, not the ranking math.
required_receipt: live probe: turn-2 follow-up shows the accepted doc entering candidates via the anchor prior in diagnostics, without turn-1 restating

section: P1.3 Add Conversation And Open-Book Anchoring
verbatim: Use anchors as ranking priors, never absolute filters.
action: Enforce priors-not-filters: implement anchors strictly as rank priors (the existing anchor-confidence pattern) and add an asserting test that non-anchored documents remain retrievable when they outscore anchored ones.
why: an anchor that becomes a filter turns follow-up retrieval into an echo chamber and kills external-evidence discovery (L618)
feasibility_note: Consistent with the P0.3 seat contract ('never a score bonus... never inflate a score to protect a seat', reservation_policy.py:19-24) — the codebase already has the right pattern to copy: selection reasons and priors, not hard filters.
required_receipt: pytest: with anchors active, a higher-scoring non-anchored doc still seats; green

section: P1.3 Add Conversation And Open-Book Anchoring
verbatim: Preserve room for adjacent/counterbalancing sources.
action: Reserve headroom: ensure anchoring priors cannot consume the seats reserve_shelf_roles reserves for adjacent/bridge/counterbalance candidates — anchored docs compete for unprotected positions only.
why: counterbalance and adjacency are the librarian's value-add; anchors must not crowd them out
feasibility_note: Sequencing: cheapest after S8 turns shelf_reserve seats live, which is why the plan slots P1.3 at S9. The seat-protection machinery (protected_keys, weakest_unprotected_pos) is shared and already handles exactly this competition pattern for the corpus floor.
required_receipt: test: anchored session + counterbalance-triggering query still seats the counterbalance doc; shelf_reserve.seated[] shows it

section: P1.3 Add Conversation And Open-Book Anchoring
verbatim: Test pronouns, "what else does this author say?", and follow-up comparison.
action: Build the conversation test battery: pronoun reference into the open doc, 'what else does this author say?' (requires S2 author metadata!), and follow-up comparison; add any new eval questions through freeze_heldout_eval so hashes stay frozen.
where: backend/tests/; backend/evals/heldout_questions.jsonl (3 followup rows exist); backend/scripts/freeze_heldout_eval.py
why: these three interaction shapes are the whole observable surface of P1.3 — untested means unbuilt
feasibility_note: RISK: 'what else does this author say' is dead on arrival until S2/T-HOOK-3 author capture lands — verified data state shows author populated on 0-1 documents per corpus (PLAN_CRITIQUE section 2). S9-after-S2 ordering already handles this; do not test it before S2's backfill.
required_receipt: conversation e2e suite green incl. author follow-up on a post-S2 corpus; new eval rows re-frozen (hash_count updated)

section: P1.3 Add Conversation And Open-Book Anchoring
verbatim: Follow-ups retain the open document while still finding necessary external evidence.
action: Acceptance A/B on the followup-shape held-out rows: follow-up turns must retain the open document (anchor prior visible in diagnostics) AND still pull required external evidence (expected_doc_ids beyond the anchored doc).
who: script: run_heldout_eval.py with history-bearing rows + compare harness
where: backend/evals/heldout_questions.jsonl (followup rows carry history field); docs/baselines/EVAL_*.json
why: this is the balance point of P1.3 — retention without echo-chamber
feasibility_note: The suite schema already carries history(optional) per registry_eval, so followup rows can express multi-turn ground truth; only 3 followup rows exist — consider adding more via the freeze path before judging.
required_receipt: EVAL artifact: followup subset shows anchored-doc retention AND external expected docs hit, vs baseline

section: P1.3 Add Conversation And Open-Book Anchoring
verbatim: Sensitive personal traits are never silently inferred or persisted.
action: Adopt as a standing invariant with an audit test: maintain a sensitive-trait denylist/no-inference policy for profile writes; seed a conversation implying sensitive traits and assert zero persisted profile fields result and the inspection API shows nothing.
who: junior-agent (policy + audit test); owner signs the sensitive-trait class list
where: backend/services/conversation.py; profile store write path; backend/tests/
why: silent inference of sensitive traits is a trust-destroying failure with no rollback — it must be structurally impossible, not just avoided
feasibility_note: Cheapest enforced at the single profile-store write path L609 creates (allowlist of signal classes rather than denylist of traits is the robust construction). This is an invariant from birth of the store, not a feature added later.
required_receipt: audit pytest green: seeded sensitive-trait conversation → zero persisted profile fields; runs in CI from S9 onward

section: P1.4 Route Shelves Before Books
verbatim: Define a versioned corpus/shelf profile card derived from document profiles, concepts, provenance, freshness, and readiness.
action: Define corpus_card.v0: a versioned per-corpus profile schema aggregated from per-doc librarian_card.v0 / slim_card_payload projections + document profiles + provenance/freshness (post-S2 bibliographic) + readiness (post-S6 split); document it before building the router.
who: junior-agent (schema + builder); owner reviews the schema
where: new backend/services/librarian/ corpus-card module; Mongo (new collection); backend/services/librarian/card_builder.py:598 (slim_card_payload as aggregation source)
why: shelf-before-book routing needs a truthful per-corpus summary or it routes on vibes
feasibility_note: Feasible mechanically but sequencing-critical: building corpus cards before S2 (bibliographic ~0 everywhere, V) and S5 (latent rollup) repeats critique C2 — a card built while its feed is empty. TERMINOLOGY: registry_librarian — 'card' names THREE artifacts (librarian_card.v0 per-doc [built, 673 live], THIS per-corpus card [PLAN-ONLY, zero code], P3.2 bridge cards [PLAN-ONLY]); and 'shelf' here means CORPUS, not shelf role. P1.4 has no explicit S row — earliest honest slot is S8 riding the Tier-0 slim-projection pass, after S2/S5/S6.
required_receipt: corpus_card.v0 schema doc + builder producing cards for all 4 corpora with non-empty provenance/freshness/readiness fields (post-S2/S6 data)

section: P1.4 Route Shelves Before Books
verbatim: Index corpus cards with distinct kind and corpus ownership.
action: Index corpus cards with a distinct point kind and explicit corpus ownership so they can never be confused with doc summaries or child points; extend the qdrant_writer payload contract accordingly.
where: backend/services/storage/qdrant_writer.py; Qdrant (kind-tagged points or dedicated collection); Mongo corpus-card collection
why: the owner architecture reserves doc_summaries for summary vectors only (Tier-0) — corpus cards need their own kind to not pollute that contract
feasibility_note: Straight extension of the existing kind-discipline: librarian_card.v0's slim Tier-0 payload is planned-but-unwritten to Qdrant (registry_librarian: 'NO card payload exists yet'), so the doc-card and corpus-card projections can land under one indexing design pass instead of two.
required_receipt: Qdrant scroll filtered by the new kind returns only corpus cards, each carrying corpus ownership; doc_summaries collection untouched

section: P1.4 Route Shelves Before Books
verbatim: Route unscoped queries to high-recall candidate shelves before document routing.
action: Build the shelf-first router: for unscoped queries, score corpus cards first and emit a high-recall candidate shelf set that document routing then operates within; wire it ahead of Tier-0 document routing.
where: new router in backend/services/retriever/; Tier-0 routing layer; corpus cards
why: top-down retrieval (corpus/shelf → document → hierarchy) is a non-negotiable invariant; today unscoped queries skip the shelf stage entirely
feasibility_note: PLAN-ONLY per registry_librarian ('no collection, no Qdrant kind, no router exists'); high-recall bias is the right v0 posture — the acceptance (L635) punishes recall loss more than fan-out. Depends on L624/L626.
required_receipt: diagnostics on an unscoped query showing shelf candidate set produced BEFORE document routing, with per-shelf scores

section: P1.4 Route Shelves Before Books
verbatim: Preserve user-selected corpora even when shelf routing is weak.
action: Re-verify rather than build: user-selected corpora preservation is ALREADY implemented as the corpus floor (ranking_policy.py:1682; final_selector.reserve_corpora:312) and the user-selected corpus boundary is a non-negotiable invariant; add a regression test in the shelf-routing PR asserting selection survives weak shelf scores.
who: script: pytest in the shelf-routing PR
why: shelf routing must never override an explicit user scope — that boundary is the trust contract of corpus selection
feasibility_note: registry_librarian shelf-routing row states this checklist line 'is ALREADY implemented as the corpus floor' for the current pipeline; the only new work is proving the property still holds once the new router exists (routing happens upstream of the floor).
required_receipt: pytest: user-selected corpus with weak shelf score still contributes evidence via corpus reservation; green in the shelf-routing PR

section: P1.4 Route Shelves Before Books
verbatim: Add fallback fan-out when shelf confidence is ambiguous.
action: Implement confidence-gated fallback: when shelf scores are ambiguous (below margin or flat), fan out to all permitted corpora instead of trusting the router; log the fallback per silent-fallback accounting.
why: a confident-but-wrong shelf router silently deletes recall; ambiguity must degrade to today's behavior, not to a guess
feasibility_note: Fallback-to-current-behavior is the cheap, safe construction; the mandatory part is counting it (memory: every fallback counts+surfaces its rate) so a 100%-fallback router can't masquerade as shipped.
required_receipt: diagnostics field shelf_routing.fallback=true on an ambiguous query + a surfaced fallback-rate counter over the held-out run

section: P1.4 Route Shelves Before Books
verbatim: Surface the shelves searched and skipped.
action: Surface shelves searched and skipped (with scores/reasons) in retrieval diagnostics, mirroring the shelf_reserve seated[]/skipped{} diagnostic pattern already live in trace metadata.
why: transparency is what makes shelf routing auditable and its recall losses debuggable
feasibility_note: Direct copy of an existing pattern: selector_meta['shelf_reserve'] → retrieval_diagnostics (final_selector.py:596, chat_orchestrator.py:7371) shows exactly how to surface a {considered, seated, skipped, reasons} structure.
required_receipt: live chat trace showing shelves_searched[] and shelves_skipped[] with reasons on an unscoped query

section: P1.4 Route Shelves Before Books
verbatim: Unscoped queries reduce unnecessary corpus fan-out without recall loss.
action: Acceptance A/B: run the held-out suite router-on vs router-off; require reduced corpus fan-out on unscoped queries (fewer corpora searched) with zero doc-recall loss at preregistered thresholds.
why: fan-out reduction is the router's whole efficiency claim; recall preservation is its safety claim — both must be numbers
feasibility_note: Needs the L583 compare harness plus a fan-out counter in diagnostics; the cross_corpus (6) and broad (4) shape rows are the sensitive subset.
required_receipt: EVAL diff: mean corpora-searched down on unscoped rows, doc-hit delta >= 0, thresholds preregistered

section: P1.4 Route Shelves Before Books
verbatim: Cross-domain questions still discover non-obvious but defensible shelves.
action: Acceptance check on cross-domain rows: verify the router still surfaces non-obvious but defensible shelves (shelf appears with an explainable card-field overlap, not a topic rule); note the suite has only 2 cross_domain rows — add more via the freeze path if the sample is too thin to judge.
who: script: eval + manual owner review of shelf explanations
why: the librarian's cross-domain serendipity must survive the efficiency win of L635
feasibility_note: Judging 'defensible' requires the shelf card-field overlap to be exposed in diagnostics (L631) — sequencing the transparency work first makes this acceptance cheap. 2 rows is a weak sample; expanding it is firewall-safe via freeze_heldout_eval.
required_receipt: per-row artifact: each cross_domain question lists the non-obvious shelf found + the card fields that justified it

section: P1.5 Implement Librarian Selection Roles
verbatim: Add retrieval roles: direct, foundational, adjacent, bridge, and counterbalance. (merged + live-probed 2026-07-13 on the PoC pair: 3 direct / 2 adjacent / 2 bridges with evidence chains / policy-triggered counterbalance; foundational honestly skipped)
receipt: merged + live-probed 2026-07-13; PoC pair: 3 direct / 2 adjacent / 2 bridges / counterbalance; foundational skipped
action: Accept receipt (merged + live-probed 2026-07-13) — the five roles exist as code (assign_shelf_roles, shelf_engine.py:368; per-role builders :185-341); re-verify anytime via `python backend/scripts/probe_shelf_roles.py` (read-only dry-run over live librarian_cards).
why: the role vocabulary is the librarian's selection grammar — everything in P1.5 hangs off it
feasibility_note: registry_librarian confirms all five _role_* builders + the live-probe receipt (3 direct / 2 adjacent / 2 bridges / policy counterbalance / foundational honestly skipped). Canonical term: 'shelf role' (say shelf role for P1.5; reserve bare 'shelf' for the P1.4 corpus meaning).
required_receipt: probe_shelf_roles.py output on the PoC pair showing role assignments + policy_version, exit 0

section: P1.5 Implement Librarian Selection Roles
verbatim: Assign roles per query; never stamp a document with one permanent shelf role. (merged + live-probed 2026-07-13 on the PoC pair)
action: Accept receipt — per-query assignment is structural: roles are computed by assign_shelf_roles(query_concepts, cards) and never persisted ('none persisted (query-relative by contract)'); re-verify by grepping for any persisted role field on documents/cards (should be zero).
why: a permanent shelf role would fossilize one query's perspective onto every future query
feasibility_note: Verified by contract shape: the engine is a pure function of (query_concepts, cards); registry_librarian store_fields confirm nothing persisted. The invariant is upheld by architecture, not discipline — the strongest form.
required_receipt: grep/Mongo scan showing no role field persisted on librarian_cards or documents

section: P1.5 Implement Librarian Selection Roles
verbatim: Derive roles from universal capabilities, mechanisms, problems, risks, and source evidence rather than topic-specific rules.
action: Close the universality gap at S8: eligibility already reads only universal card fields (capabilities_developed, mechanisms_taught, transferable_principles, central_subjects) — the real gap is upstream feed quality (pair cards built on zero latent_concepts, critique C2). After S4 capture + S5 rollup, rebuild cards and audit that no topic-specific rule has crept into shelf_engine; keep trigger vocabularies in versioned policy data (shelf_policy.v0), never Python conditionals.
why: topic rules would silently turn the librarian into a hand-tuned demo that only works on the PoC pair
feasibility_note: The code side is already universal-shaped (indexed field overlap only, registry_librarian); satisfying this claim is mostly the S4/S5/S8 data program plus an audit. shelf_policy_data.py:1-16 'DATA, not logic' is the sanctioned home for curated vocabularies per the no-hardcoded-topic-rules invariant.
required_receipt: post-S8 probe_shelf_roles.py on a NON-PoC corpus (UGO) showing sensible role assignments + a code audit note confirming zero topic-specific conditionals in shelf_engine

section: P1.5 Implement Librarian Selection Roles
verbatim: Define deterministic v0 eligibility: Direct = central-subject overlap; Foundational = capability overlap with mechanisms/evidence; Adjacent = shared capabilities, different subjects; Bridge = shared transferable-principle IDs + evidence both sides; Counterbalance = versioned policy trigger + evidence-backed concept.
action: Accept receipt — the five deterministic v0 eligibility rules exist verbatim in code (_role_direct:185, _role_foundational:216 with FOUNDATIONAL_MIN_CAPABILITY_OVERLAP=0.25, _role_adjacent:253, _role_bridge:289, _role_counterbalance:341 under shelf_policy.v0); re-verify via probe.
why: deterministic eligibility is what keeps role seating auditable and LLM-free per the seat invariant
feasibility_note: registry_librarian evidence: shelf_engine docstrings match the checklist eligibility wording 'verbatim in intent'; counterbalance is policy-DATA-triggered (COUNTERBALANCE_KEYS, POLICY_VERSION='shelf_policy.v0') exactly as claimed.
required_receipt: probe output showing per-role eligibility reasons on the PoC pair; policy_version stamped shelf_policy.v0

section: P1.5 Implement Librarian Selection Roles
verbatim: Require every bridge recommendation to expose: document -> concept -> transferable principle -> user goal. (merged + live-probed 2026-07-13 on the PoC pair)
action: Accept receipt — live probe showed 2 bridges with evidence chains; re-verify that every bridge assignment exposes the full document→concept→transferable-principle→user-goal chain in its assignment payload, and keep the chain mandatory in the S8 rebuild A/B.
why: the evidence chain is what separates a bridge recommendation from a random cross-domain association (L676)
feasibility_note: Receipt live-probed 2026-07-13; bridge eligibility requires shared transferable-principle IDs + evidence on both sides by construction. Chain quality will improve materially after S4/S5 fill latent/principle feeds — worth re-probing post-S8.
required_receipt: probe output: each bridge row carries {doc_id, concept, transferable_principle_id, user_goal} chain, none missing

section: P1.5 Implement Librarian Selection Roles
verbatim: Treat embedding scores as candidate recall only; they cannot independently satisfy any shelf-role gate. (merged + live-probed 2026-07-13 on the PoC pair)
action: Accept receipt and keep as invariant — embedding scores gate nothing in shelf_engine (eligibility is indexed-field overlap only); re-assert with a test that a high-cosine/zero-overlap candidate is never seated by any role.
why: mirrors the non-negotiable invariant: embeddings recall candidates; only deterministic evidence-backed policy grants seats
feasibility_note: Structural: assign_shelf_roles takes (query_concepts, cards) and no vector scores at all, so embeddings CANNOT satisfy a role gate. The claim is upheld by the function signature — the cheapest possible enforcement.
required_receipt: pytest: candidate with no card-field overlap is skipped regardless of retrieval score; green

section: P1.5 Implement Librarian Selection Roles
verbatim: Deduplicate by document while retaining multiple validated roles, themes, and reasons. (merged + live-probed 2026-07-13 on the PoC pair)
action: Accept receipt — dedup-by-document with multi-role retention was live-probed 2026-07-13; re-verify that a doc qualifying for two roles appears once with both roles/reasons retained in the assignment output.
who: script: probe_shelf_roles.py with a multi-role fixture query
why: losing secondary roles would silently discard the reasons diversity the reading path needs
feasibility_note: Part of the merged live-probed engine contract ({assignments, shelf_counts, skipped_roles, policy_version}); no contrary evidence in registries.
required_receipt: probe output: one doc, two roles, both reasons present; no duplicate doc rows

section: P1.5 Implement Librarian Selection Roles
verbatim: Reserve role diversity only when candidates clear relevance/evidence gates. [IN CODE — wave3/reserve, dark behind SHELF_RESERVE_ENABLED, pending before/after evals]
receipt: wave3/reserve; SHELF_RESERVE_ENABLED flag; pending before/after evals
action: Run the S8 A/B: flip SHELF_RESERVE_ENABLED (config.py:392, default False) in a canary, run the held-out suite vs the 2026-07-13 eval-baseline (subset to the common 56 ids), and confirm seats are granted only through passes_corpus_reservation-gated candidates (P0.3 reservation_policy, never an unconditional floor).
who: junior-agent (A/B run); owner reads the diff before enabling by default
why: role-diversity seats are only worth having if they don't displace better evidence — that is exactly what the A/B measures
feasibility_note: Code is fully built and dark (wave3/reserve, merge f049041). NOTE: PLAN_CRITIQUE:71 ('shelf_reserve as a symbol does not exist in backend code') is STALE since that merge — registry_librarian flags it; do not re-derive plans from that line. Near-name collision: shelf_reserve (P1.5 seat pass) vs shelf routing (P1.4) are unrelated concepts.
required_receipt: EVAL diff artifact shelf_reserve on/off: no doc-hit regression, seated[]/skipped{} populated, all seats show passes_corpus_reservation lineage

section: P1.5 Implement Librarian Selection Roles
verbatim: Skip a role seat when no candidate qualifies; never fill a quota with weak evidence. (merged + live-probed 2026-07-13 on the PoC pair; foundational honestly skipped)
action: Accept receipt — seat-skipping is live (foundational honestly skipped on the PoC pair; skipped_roles is a first-class engine output and shelf_reserve caps at 1 seat/role, pool-only); re-verify skipped_roles reasons surface in diagnostics.
why: quota-filling with weak evidence is the classic failure that turns a librarian into a horoscope
feasibility_note: The strongest evidence is behavioral: the live probe DID skip foundational rather than force a weak candidate — the honest-skip path is exercised, not just written.
required_receipt: probe/trace output containing skipped_roles with per-role reasons on a query where a role has no qualifier

section: P1.5 Implement Librarian Selection Roles
verbatim: Return a reading path, not merely a flat chunk list, for librarian-mode questions. [IN CODE — wave3/reserve (diagnostics seed: meta.shelf_reserve.reading_path), dark behind SHELF_RESERVE_ENABLED, pending before/after evals]
action: Two-stage: (1) at S8, A/B the diagnostics seed (meta.shelf_reserve.reading_path — role-ordered {doc_id, corpus_id, role} list, final_selector.py:394,523-537, surfaced chat_orchestrator.py:7364-7371); (2) build the user-facing render — the answer-shaping half is to-be-built, nearest seam the frontend live-trace tabs.
why: a reading path is the librarian's actual product for librarian-mode questions; a flat chunk list is just search
feasibility_note: registry_librarian 'reading path' row is OVERLAPPING: plan wants user-facing, code has only the dark diagnostics SEED. Do not mark done at seed-A/B — the claim's own wording ('return a reading path... for librarian-mode questions') requires the user-facing half.
required_receipt: (1) A/B trace showing reading_path populated and ordered direct→foundational→adjacent→bridge→counterbalance; (2) frontend rendering it on a librarian-mode question

section: P1.5 Implement Librarian Selection Roles
verbatim: Non-obvious recommendations are explainable and source-grounded.
action: Score explainability in the S8 A/B via the L568 rubric: every non-direct seat must carry its eligibility reason (card-field overlap, principle id, policy trigger) into the trace, and rubric review must judge the explanation defensible.
why: explainable seating is what lets a user (and the owner) trust a non-obvious recommendation
feasibility_note: Reasons already ride the engine output (assignments carry eligibility bases); the open work is the rubric (L568) that turns 'explainable' from vibes into a scored property. Depends on L568.
required_receipt: EVAL artifact: 100% of seated non-direct docs carry machine-readable reasons; rubric explainability subscore above preregistered bar

section: P1.5 Implement Librarian Selection Roles
verbatim: Serendipity does not become random semantic association.
action: Enforce and measure: serendipity candidates (adjacent/bridge) must clear evidence gates (shared capabilities / principle IDs with evidence both sides) — assert in the S8 A/B that zero seats exist whose only justification is vector similarity.
who: script: eval assertion over seat lineage
why: random semantic association is the failure mode the deterministic gates were built to prevent — this acceptance is its regression alarm
feasibility_note: Structurally guaranteed today (L659: embeddings can't satisfy gates); the acceptance still earns its keep as a standing check because Phase-4 LLM enrichment will later add pressure to loosen gates.
required_receipt: eval assertion green: every adjacent/bridge seat lists card-field/principle evidence; none seated on similarity alone

section: P1.5 Implement Librarian Selection Roles
verbatim: Counterbalancing sources are included for manipulation, bias, or safety risks where relevant.
action: Verify counterbalance inclusion on risk-bearing queries in the S8 A/B: policy-triggered (HIGH_MISUSE_KEYS → COUNTERBALANCE_KEYS under shelf_policy.v0) counterbalance seats appear for manipulation/bias/safety queries where a qualifying evidence-backed doc exists; track the known limitation that librarian_cards.counterbalancing_concepts is hardwired empty in v0 (card_builder.py:81) — role assignment reads central_subjects + policy keys instead.
who: script: eval on policy-trigger rows; owner curates policy vocab versions
why: counterbalance on manipulation/bias/safety topics is the librarian's duty-of-care feature
feasibility_note: The live probe already produced a policy-triggered counterbalance seat, so the path works. TERMINOLOGY (registry_librarian): three artifacts share the 'counterbalance' stem — the role (per-query), the policy keys (versioned data), and the card field (persisted, ALWAYS EMPTY in v0, effectively PLAN-ONLY until Phase 4); keep them distinct or Phase 4 double-implements the qualifier source.
required_receipt: eval rows with manipulation/bias/safety phrasing show a counterbalance seat with policy trigger + evidence, or an honest skip reason when no doc qualifies

section: P1.6 Route By Answer Shape
verbatim: Formalize definition, procedure, enumeration, comparison, relationship, broad synthesis, recommendation, and decision-support policies.
action: Formalize the eight shape policies as a versioned policy table consumed by the EXISTING answer_shape pipeline — classifier _answer_shape (query_plan.py:650), coverage_threshold by shape (answerability_tuning.py:85), broad_document_routing (retriever/__init__.py:1452), prioritize_enumeration_candidates (planned_fusion.py:1187) — do NOT add a second shape classifier; unify the answer_shape/answer_type field-name drift when touching the probe contract.
why: a shape-keyed policy table turns four scattered heuristics into one auditable routing contract
feasibility_note: registry_retrieval 'answer shape' row: OVERLAPPING — classifier + threshold calibration + breadth/enumeration heuristics EXIST; formal policies are the open half. Mild collision: same value stored as answer_shape (plan) and answer_type (RetrievalProbe, query_plan.py:283) — unify on answer_shape.
required_receipt: versioned shape-policy table in code/data + diagnostics showing the policy id applied per query; existing shape tests still green

section: P1.6 Route By Answer Shape
verbatim: Allow validated summaries/themes as synthesis scaffolds for broad queries.
action: Allow validated summaries/themes as synthesis scaffolds for broad-shape queries: only modeled abstractive summaries (summary_model non-empty) qualify; scaffold selection must respect the owner architecture (parent summaries are never embedded except as the doc_summaries Tier-0 lane).
why: broad questions need document-level scaffolding to gain diversity (L695) without drowning in child chunks
feasibility_note: Substrate is healthy post-P0.1 (verify PASSES all 4 corpora, 0 empty-model, PLAN_CRITIQUE section 2), so 'validated' is enforceable via summary_model + validation status per the invariant that no extractive placeholder poses as abstractive.
required_receipt: broad-shape query trace showing summary/theme scaffold entries flagged as scaffolds with summary_model present, plus hydrated evidence beneath them

section: P1.6 Route By Answer Shape
verbatim: Hydrate child/parent evidence before treating a generated summary as a citation.
action: Enforce hydrate-before-cite: any generated summary used in the answer must first hydrate its child/parent evidence (hydrate_chunks / parent_hydration) so citations point at source chunks, never at the generated text; align with the dark WATERFALL_ASSEMBLY hydration waterfall (config.py:290) rather than building a second assembly rule.
where: backend/services/retriever/ (hydration); backend/config.py:290 (WATERFALL_ASSEMBLY); citation assembly in chat_orchestrator.py
why: citing an unhydrated generated summary is fabricated provenance — the exact trust failure the summary-integrity program exists to prevent
feasibility_note: Two existing seams cover this: parent_hydration (canonical per registry_retrieval) and the owner-architecture deterministic waterfall (full→summary→skip) already dark behind WATERFALL_ASSEMBLY awaiting its A/B gate — implement this claim as a rule INSIDE that waterfall to honor one-migration-per-seam.
required_receipt: test: answer citing summary-derived content resolves every citation to a hydrated child/parent chunk id; a summary with failed hydration is demoted, not cited

section: P1.6 Route By Answer Shape
verbatim: Use sibling expansion for structured list/enumeration nodes.
action: Extend enumeration handling to sibling completion: when a list/enumeration query lands on a structured node, expand to its sibling set via summary-tree structure (parent/child ids) instead of stopping at the vector hits; build on prioritize_enumeration_candidates rather than beside it.
why: list answers are judged by completeness — vector recall alone systematically drops middle siblings
feasibility_note: P0.2 hierarchy repair (in progress) is the data dependency: sibling expansion is only as good as the parent/child linkage it walks. The enumeration special-casing seam already exists; this is an extension, not a new lane.
required_receipt: list-shape query over a known structured doc returns the full sibling set (fixture-validated), diagnostics showing sibling_expansion fired

section: P1.6 Route By Answer Shape
verbatim: Use dynamic breadth/K based on score cliffs, obligations, and answer shape.
action: Implement dynamic breadth/K keyed on score cliffs, coverage obligations, and answer shape; coordinate with the P1.9 adaptive-rerank 'budget-by-cliff' item so cliff detection is built once and consumed by both breadth and rerank budgeting.
where: backend/services/retriever/ranking_policy.py; backend/services/retriever/__init__.py (K selection); planned P1.9 rerank budget seam
why: fixed K over-retrieves easy questions (latency) and under-retrieves broad ones (recall) — the cliff is the honest signal
feasibility_note: RISK: interacts with reranker admission and the Metal GPU contention constraint (never rerank parallel support passes) — dynamic K must respect the rerank latency envelope or it silently reintroduces the 30s-cadence pathology. Build cliff detection as one shared utility per one-migration-per-seam spirit.
required_receipt: A/B: latency down on single_fact/direct shapes, recall flat-or-up on broad/list shapes, with per-shape K distribution in diagnostics

section: P1.6 Route By Answer Shape
verbatim: List questions retrieve complete sibling sets when source structure supports them.
action: Acceptance on the 6 list-shape held-out rows: sibling sets retrieved complete where source structure supports them (fixture-known ground truth), measured in the S9 A/B against the eval-baseline.
why: completeness on list questions is the observable proof that L687 works end-to-end
feasibility_note: Requires expected_all_docs semantics (already in the suite schema) so partial sibling recall scores as partial — the suite is built for exactly this measurement.
required_receipt: EVAL diff: list-shape subset shows sibling-set completeness improvement, no regression elsewhere

section: P1.6 Route By Answer Shape
verbatim: Broad questions gain document diversity without sacrificing evidence.
action: Acceptance on the 4 broad-shape held-out rows: document diversity up (distinct docs in final evidence) with evidence coverage held, measured in the S9 A/B; use the diversity metric already captured in the baseline run.
why: diversity without evidence is padding; evidence without diversity is tunnel vision — broad synthesis needs both
feasibility_note: 4 broad rows is a thin sample for a diversity claim — consider adding rows via the freeze path before judging. The scaffold work (L684) is the mechanism expected to move this number; test them together.
required_receipt: EVAL diff: broad-shape subset distinct-doc count up, evidence-coverage metric flat-or-up, thresholds preregistered
